feature: Add structural risk axis: architecturalHub as a Phase-4 blast-radius amplifier + find_cycles structural section, codegraph-gated; expand rules/references/signal-interpretation.md with codegraph pair-diagnostics (fanIn/isHub/instability/transitiveImpact/cycle membership).
detection_signal: prime '## Enrichment' lists codegraph.symbols when ON; ENV-A omits it AND find_cycles is not registered. architecturalHub is a rerank preset (always callable) but degrades to similarity when codegraph OFF.

evals.name: integration-hub-amplifier-and-cycles
evals.prompt: [ENV-B: prime lists codegraph.symbols + codegraph.file.fanIn thresholds] Assess risk in domains/ingest.
evals.expected: Runs the existing 4-preset churn scan (bugHunt/hotspots/techDebt/dangerous) AND adds the structural axis: architecturalHub as a Phase-4 amplifier escalating any Critical/High churn candidate that is also a high-fanIn hub (blast-radius), plus a find_cycles structural-risk section.
evals.failure_mode: Churn scan only; no architecturalHub amplifier, no find_cycles — structural risk invisible.

assertions.text: runs the 4 git-signal presets (existing flow preserved)
uses architecturalHub to escalate a churny high-fanIn file as blast-radius risk (amplifier, NOT a 5th overlap preset)
runs find_cycles and reports cycles as a structural risk dimension

evals.name: integration-explicit-cycles
evals.prompt: [ENV-B] Evaluate the code health of the whole project, including any circular dependencies.
evals.expected: Stratified 4-preset broad scan + find_cycles scope=file (with noise guard >20 -> narrow) surfaced as its own structural section.

assertions.text: still runs the stratified churn scan for broad scope (no regression)

evals.name: classification-codegraph-pair-diagnostics
evals.prompt: [ENV-B] Is adapters/qdrant-client.ts a god module / coupling point or a bug attractor?
evals.expected: Disambiguate using codegraph fanIn/isHub (high fanIn + isHub -> coupling/god module; low fanIn + high bugFixRate -> bug attractor), per the expanded signal-interpretation. codegraph fanIn supersedes the imports proxy when available.
evals.failure_mode: Uses only the imports proxy / churn; ignores codegraph fanIn/isHub pair-diagnostic.

assertions.text: uses codegraph fanIn / isHub (not just the imports proxy) to disambiguate god-module vs bug-attractor
states codegraph fanIn supersedes the imports proxy where present

evals.name: off-guard-hub-amplifier
evals.prompt: [ENV-A: prime has no codegraph.symbols; find_cycles not in tool list] Assess risk in domains/ingest.
evals.expected: 4-preset churn scan runs normally. architecturalHub amplifier is skipped or explicitly noted as degraded (no fanIn signals -> similarity); find_cycles section skipped with an honest note. No fabricated 'structural hub' from a degraded preset.
evals.failure_mode: Presents architecturalHub similarity output as a real blast-radius hub, or claims a structural section it cannot compute.

assertions.text: the 4 git-signal presets still run (churn risk map unaffected)
does NOT present a degraded architecturalHub result as a real fanIn hub
skips find_cycles and notes structural/cycle analysis needs codegraph (does NOT claim 'no cycles')

evals.name: off-guard-cycles-no-false-clean
evals.prompt: [ENV-A] Is there any circular-dependency risk in domains/trajectory?
evals.expected: Detect codegraph OFF; find_cycles not registered. Say cycle detection needs codegraph; do NOT claim 'no circular dependencies'.
evals.failure_mode: Reports 'no circular-dependency risk' as if verified.

assertions.text: detects codegraph OFF and states cycle detection is unavailable
does NOT claim 'no cycles / clean' when it cannot compute them

evals.name: control-no-regression-broad
evals.prompt: [ENV-B] Assess risk across the whole project.
evals.expected: Existing flow preserved: stratified second scan with !**/dominant-domain/**, negativeIds dedup MERGE, pair-diagnostics report. Structural axis is ADDITIVE, not a replacement.
evals.failure_mode: Structural axis displaces the stratified churn MERGE.

assertions.text: runs the stratified second scan (dominant-domain exclusion) — core flow intact
structural axis is additive on top of the churn MERGE, not a replacement

evals.name: edge-no-hubs-no-cycles
evals.prompt: [ENV-B] Assess risk in src/core/utils (a flat leaf module with no hubs and no cycles).
evals.expected: Churn scan runs; architecturalHub finds no isHub files -> say so; find_cycles empty -> 'no cycles (DAG)' is valid because codegraph is ON. No fabricated structural risk.
evals.failure_mode: Invents a structural hub or cycle where none exist.

assertions.text: honestly reports no architectural hubs in a flat module
treats empty find_cycles as a real DAG (codegraph ON) — no fabricated cycle

evals.name: subagent-delegated-pathpattern
evals.prompt: [ENV-B] [subagent: explore delegated a pathPattern] Run risk-assessment on pathPattern **/domains/language/**.
evals.expected: Uses the delegated pathPattern as-is (scopeType=domain, skip resolution), runs the 4-preset scan + architecturalHub amplifier + find_cycles scoped to that pathPattern.
evals.failure_mode: Re-resolves scope, or drops the structural axis in a delegated/subagent context.

assertions.text: uses the delegated pathPattern directly and scopes find_cycles/architecturalHub to it
includes the structural axis even in the delegated subagent flow
